sections.paragraph:
ὀρεχθέω (ὀρεχθέω,)
Etym. only in pres. and epic imperf. ὀρέχθεον

either to stretch oneself or struggle in the throes of death (from ὀρέγομαι), or (akin to ροχθέω), to gasp in the death-ruckle, Il.; of the heart, to palpitate, Ar.; of the sea, to stretch itself, i. e. roll up, to the beach, Theocr. (in doric inf. ὀρεχθῆν).
